title: armeria vulgaris subsp. arctica
intro: Armeria vulgaris subsp. arctica, also known as Arctic sea pink or Arctic thale cress, is a subspecies of the perennial plant Armeria maritima that is native to the Arctic tundra. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Armeria vulgaris subsp. arctica include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
